antipattern.symptom: Test asserts that a transaction.on_commit callback fired or that a Celery task was enqueued via post_save; assertion fails. The same code works in production.
antipattern.root-cause: Test uses the `db` fixture, which wraps everything in a savepoint that is rolled back after the test. on_commit hooks never fire because no real commit happens; post_save signals fire on save() but the side-effect handler (e.g., transaction.on_commit) doesn't.
antipattern.fix: Use `transactional_db` for tests asserting on real-commit behaviour. Spec rule rejects db_fixture=db on fixtures whose names suggest signals / on_commit / select_for_update.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass alone but fail when the suite runs in a different order. Some tests see modified data because an earlier test mutated a session-scoped fixture.
antipattern.root-cause: Session-scoped fixture returns a mutable model instance; tests modify it. The mutations accumulate across the session.
antipattern.fix: Session scope ONLY for immutable static reference data. Spec rule forbids scope=session with mutable=true.

antipattern.symptom: First CI run with pytest-xdist + --reuse-db fails with "database already exists" errors from multiple workers.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple xdist workers race to create the test database on cold start. --reuse-db assumes the DB already exists.
antipattern.fix: Use --create-db on cold start (first run, schema changes); switch to --reuse-db on subsequent runs. The spec's xdist.cold_start_flag and warm_flag document the convention.

antipattern.symptom: A setting change in one test leaks into the next test that depends on the default. Tests pass in isolation, fail in the suite.
antipattern.root-cause: `@override_settings` was used as a decorator inside a session-scoped fixture. Its scope doesn't compose cleanly across pytest fixture scopes.
antipattern.fix: Use pytest-django's built-in `settings` fixture (`settings.FOO = 'bar'`). It is properly scoped per-test and cleaned up automatically.

antipattern.symptom: Test upload creates a file in the repo's MEDIA_ROOT. The file persists between runs; next test sees stale data; git status shows new files.
antipattern.root-cause: MEDIA_ROOT in test settings points at the same location as dev/prod. Tests that upload files write to the real disk.
antipattern.fix: Autouse fixture using tmp_path: `settings.MEDIA_ROOT = str(tmp_path / 'media')`. Spec settings_overrides.MEDIA_ROOT_per_test=true is required.
